retry_reason: The refcount_set operation is unconditional and cannot fail; the only failure mode is memory allocation (GFP_ATOMIC) which is unlikely to succeed on immediate retry.
contract_summary: This function allocates a new aarp_entry and initializes its reference count to 1 via refcount_set, establishing the initial reference. The caller takes ownership of this reference and must eventually release it with a corresponding aarp_put (or similar decrement) to avoid leaks. The function also initializes the packet_queue. On memory allocation failure, it returns NULL; the caller must check the return value.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to allocate and initialize a new aarp_entry with a reference count set to 1. The function performs allocation and queue initialization alongside the refcount set, so it is not purely a refcount helper, but the refcount operation is a core part of the object creation.

check.reason: refcount_set(&a->refcnt, 1) sets the refcount of the freshly allocated aarp_entry. The returned pointer 'a' is the critical variable at interface (return value). The access path a.refcnt.refs correctly traces from the return object through the refcnt (refcount_t) field to the inner refs (atomic_t). The type chain aarp_entry--refcount_t--atomic_t matches the callee's type chain for refcount_set. The segment count (3) matches the type chain count (3).
